name: pynanovna

metadata.name: pynanovna
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A package to use a NanoVNA

documentation:
# *pynanovna*
![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pynanovna)
![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/pynanovna)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pynanovna)
![GitHub Repo stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/PICC-Group/pynanovna)


This is a python module for using a NanoVNA.


## 🌟 Features
    ✅ Supporting almost all NanoVNAs.
    📶 Run single sweeps of s11 & s21 data.
    🔄 Stream continuous sweeps.
    🛠️ Calibrate your NanoVNA.
    📊 Interactive, real-time plots of data.
    📁 Record to CSV files.


## 🛠️ Installation
Install with `pip install pynanovna`.

## 🚀 Example
```
import pynanovna

vna = pynanovna.VNA()

vna.set_sweep(2.0e9, 2.6e9, 101)

stream = vna.stream()
for s11, s21, frequencies in stream:
    print(s11, s21, frequencies)
```

See `examples/example.py` for a more detailed example on some use cases of the project.

See `examples/example_calibration.py` for details on how to calibrate you NanoVNA.

## 📚 API Reference
API Reference is available at [pynanovna.readthedocs.io](https://pynanovna.readthedocs.io/en/latest)

## 📖 Cite This Library

If you use *pynanovna* in your research or project, please cite it as follows:

**BibTeX**
```bibtex
@software{pynanovna,
  author = {PICC-Group, Teo Bergkvist},
  title = {pynanovna: A Python Module for NanoVNA},
  year = {2024},
  url = {https://github.com/PICC-Group/pynanovna},
  version = {1.0.0},
}
```
## 📜 Licence
*pynanovna* is under GNU General Public License.

## Contribution and feature requests
If you want to contribute to *pynanovna*, please create a pull request and assign to:
- [@tbergkvist](https://github.com/tbergkvist)
- [@OdoctorG](https://github.com/OdoctorG)

If you have a feature you think is missing and want implemented, create an issue with the `enhancement` label, describing clearly the feature.

## 🕰️ History
Originally this was the fork [nanovna-saver-headless](https://github.com/PICC-Group/nanovna-saver-headless) from [nanovna-saver](https://github.com/NanoVNA-Saver/nanovna-saver) but when that project no longer shared much code with the original we decided to create a new project.

# ⭐️ If you find *pynanovna* useful, please support us by starring the repository ⭐️

functions.name: pynanovna.utils.stream_from_csv
documentation:
Stream previously recorded data from a csv file.

Args:
    filename (string): Path to the csv file.
    sweepdivider (string): Used to identify where sweeps end and start in the csv file.
    delay (float): Used to simulate the time it takes for the vna to sweep.

Yields:
    tuple: (s11, s21, frequencies)

functions.name: pynanovna.utils.get_portinfos
documentation:
Get information about communication ports.

Returns:
    list: Port information.

functions.name: pynanovna.utils.get_interfaces
documentation:
Get all available interfaces.

Returns:
    list: Interface

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a VNA object for the NanoVNA.

Args:
    vna_index (int): If multiple NanoVNAs are connected you can specify which to use.
    logging_level (str): The level of outputs. 'critical', 'info' or 'debug'. Defaults to 'info'.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.set_sweep
documentation:
Set the sweep parameters.

Args:
    start (int): The start frequnecy.
    stop (int): The stop frequency.
    points (int): Number of points in the sweep.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.sweep
documentation:
Run a single sweep and return the data.

Args:
    overwrite_wait: Do not change if you don't know what youre doing.
                    This can be used to lower the wait in the hardware functions.

Returns:
    tuple: s11, s21, frequencies

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.stream
documentation:
Creates a data stream from the continuous sweeping.

Args:
    overwrite_wait: Do not change if you don't know what youre doing.
                    This can be used to lower the wait in the hardware functions.

Yields:
    tuple: Yields a list of data when new data is available. Each datapoint: (s11, s21, frequencies)

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.stream_to_csv
documentation:
Function to save the stream to a csv file.

Args:
    filename (str): The filename to save to.
    nr_sweeps (int): Number of sweeps to run. Defaults to 10.
    skip_start (int): The NanoVNA usually gives bad data in the beginning, therefore this data can be skipped. Defaults to 5.
    sweepdivider (str): A string to write between every sweep data to divide.

Raises:
    TypeError: If the filename is not a string.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.calibration_step
documentation:
Runs a sweep and uses the data for calibration.

Args:
    step (str): The calibration step.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.calibrate
documentation:
Calculates a calibration from the steps.

Raises:
    Exception: If the calibration is not successfully calculated.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.save_calibration
documentation:
Save the current calibration.

Args:
    filename (str): The filename for the calibration.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.load_calibration
documentation:
Load a previous calibration from a file.

Args:
    filename (str): The file containing the previous calibration.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA._apply_calibration
documentation:
Apply calibration to raw data.

Args:
    raw_s11 (np.array): s11 data.
    raw_s21 (np.array): s21 data.

Returns:
    tuple: calibrated s-parameter data.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.set_offset_delay
documentation:
Manually set offset delay. This is used in calibration.

Args:
    delay (float): The delay.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.set_vna_wait
documentation:
There is a small sleep time in the communication with the NanoVNA, which is needed.
    You can change the sleep time in order to speed up the communication.
    Beware of unexpected errors if setting this to lower than 0.05.

Args:
    wait (float): Time in seconds

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.is_connected
documentation:
Check if the NanoVNA is connected.

Returns:
    bool: If it is connected or not.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.info
documentation:
Get info about your NanoVNA and the connection to it.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary with the info.

methods.name: pynanovna.pynanovna.VNA.kill
documentation:
Disconnect the NanoVNA.

Raises:
    Exception: If the NanoVNA was not successfully disconnected.

functions.name: pynanovna.vis.plot
documentation:
Show a magnitude plot from the data.

Args:
    stream: The data stream to plot.
    axis_mode (str): 'dynamic', 'fixed', or 'first'. Default is 'dynamic'.
        'dynamic' - Axes limits adjust to data.
        'fixed' - Axes limits are fixed as per 'fixed_limits'.
        'first' - Axes limits are set based on the first data batch and kept constant.
    fixed_limits (list): A dictionary containing axis limits if axis_mode is 'fixed'.
        Example: [min_s11, max_s11, min_s21, max_s21]
    log (bool): If the magnitude should be log or not.

functions.name: pynanovna.vis.polar
documentation:
Create polar plots for S11 and S21 data.

Args:
    stream: The data stream to plot.
    normalize (bool): If True, normalize the data based on the first value.

functions.name: pynanovna.hardware.Serial.drain_serial
documentation: drain up to 64k outstanding data in the serial incoming buffer

functions.name: pynanovna.hardware.Hardware._fix_v2_hwinfo
documentation: The USB Driver for NanoVNA V2 seems to deliver an
incompatible hardware info like:
'PORTS\\VID_04B4&PID_0008\\DEMO'
This function will fix it.

functions.name: pynanovna.hardware.Hardware.get_interfaces
documentation: Get list of interfaces with VNAs connected

functions.name: pynanovna.calibration.calibration.correct_delay
documentation:
Correct delay using delayoffset.

Args:
    datapoint (complex): The datapoint.
    frequency (int): The frequency for the datapoint.
    delay (float): Delay.
    reflect (bool): Defaults to False.

Returns:
    tuple: corrected datapoint

methods.name: pynanovna.calibration.calibration.CalDataSet.insert
documentation:
Insert a datapoint in the dataset.

Args:
    name (str): Name of dataset.
    Must be one of:
        "short",
        "open",
        "load",
        "through",
        "thrurefl",
        "isolation"
    datapoint (complex): The datapoint to insert.
    frequency (int): The frequency of the datapoint.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the name is not valid.

methods.name: pynanovna.calibration.calibration.Calibration.calibration_step
documentation:
Register data from one calibration step.

Args:
    name (str): The step.
    s11 (np.array): Raw s11 data.
    s21 (np.array): Raw s21 data.
    frequencies (np.array): Frequencies of the sweep.

methods.name: pynanovna.calibration.calibration.Calibration.is_valid_1_port
documentation:
Check if the port has been calibrated.

Returns:
    bool: If the port is calibrated.

methods.name: pynanovna.calibration.calibration.Calibration.is_valid_2_port
documentation:
Check if the port has been calibrated.

Returns:
    bool: If the port is calibrated.

If possible, read frequencies already running
if not return default values
